message:
Refactor policy command rules, add redirect tracking to symbolic exec, and enrich MCP audit context

Policy engine (policy.rs): Removed the inline CommandRule table and
all associated static LazyLock regex patterns (RM_RF_ROOT, RM_RF_GENERIC,
GIT_RESET_HARD, GIT_CLEAN_FD, GIT_PUSH_FORCE, GIT_BRANCH_DELETE,
SQL_DESTRUCTIVE) along with the CommandGateDecision enum and CommandRule
struct. These patterns are now handled by the configurable policy rules
system that was introduced in the previous commit cycle, making the
hardcoded regex table redundant. The command_gate function's match-based
dispatch was simplified accordingly. Net: -171 lines of regex patterns
and boilerplate removed.

Approval system (approval.rs): The consume_with_context method now
records the actual actor kind (robot, mcp, workflow, human) in the
audit trail instead of always hardcoding "human". This was a parity
bug where robot-initiated approval consumptions were misattributed.
New test: consume_with_context_preserves_non_human_actor_kind_in_audit
verifies all actor kinds produce correct audit records.

MCP tools (mcp_tools.rs): Added structured decision context to audit
records for wa.events_annotate, wa.events_triage, wa.events_acknowledge,
wa.events_resolve, and wa.events_escalate tools. Each audit entry now
includes surface (mcp), actor_kind, tool name, action_kind, event_id,
operation, and actor_id. New helpers: serialize_mcp_audit_decision_context
and mcp_event_mutation_decision_context. Actor kind in audit records
changed from "robot" to "mcp" for MCP-initiated operations to
distinguish automation sources.

Symbolic execution (ars_symbolic_exec.rs): ParsedCommand now tracks
redirect operations via a new `redirects: Vec<(String, String)>` field
capturing (operator, target) pairs. The parse_commands function now
handles ShellToken::Redirect by looking ahead for the target word
token, supporting operators like >, >>, 2>, 2>&1, etc. The
build_command helper updated to accept and propagate redirect info.
This enables downstream policy rules to reason about output redirection
(e.g., detecting `> /etc/passwd` or `>> ~/.ssh/authorized_keys`).

ARS compiler (ars_compile.rs): Simplified matches_safety_regex by
removing the hand-rolled character class parser (char_in_class,
bracket pattern parsing) in favor of delegating to the regex crate.
The old implementation only handled simple `[charset]+` patterns and
silently allowed everything else; the new implementation correctly
validates all regex patterns.

ARS generalize (ars_generalize.rs): Simplified safety regex matching
to align with the compiler's approach.

ARS secret scan (ars_secret_scan.rs): Minor adjustments for the
simplified regex interface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
